# Opedd

> The licensing protocol for content in the age of AI. Publishers set prices, buyers get cryptographically-signed license keys, every transaction recorded on-chain.

Opedd is infrastructure for content licensing — not a marketplace. Think Stripe, but for licensing rights to articles, datasets, and archives. Publishers embed a widget or API; buyers (human or AI) pay and instantly receive a license key (format: OP-XXXX-XXXX) with on-chain proof on Base (Coinbase L2).

## What Opedd does

- Lets publishers monetize articles and archive bundles for human republication, AI training, and AI inference/RAG use cases
- Issues cryptographically-verifiable license keys with blockchain proof
- Integrates via embeddable JavaScript widget, REST API, or MCP server (for AI agents)
- Handles Stripe payments, PDF invoicing, webhook notifications, and certificate generation

## Who uses it

- Publishers: news organizations, independent journalists, newsletters, data providers
- Buyers: AI companies licensing training data, media organizations, enterprise research teams, AI agents

## License types

- human: republication and editorial rights
- ai: AI training dataset rights
- ai_inference: RAG and inference rights
- archive: site license covering all content from a publisher within a date range

## Key public endpoints

- GET /lookup-article?url={url} — check if content is licensed and get pricing
- POST /agent-purchase — purchase a license programmatically with Stripe or USDC
- GET /verify-license?key={key} — verify a license key (returns blockchain proof)
- GET /registry — browse the public license registry

## MCP server

npx opedd-mcp — lets AI assistants discover, purchase, and verify licenses mid-conversation

## Links

- Website: https://opedd.com
- MCP server: https://npmjs.com/package/opedd-mcp
- GitHub: https://github.com/Opedd
